title: Studies on Indian Medicinal Plants. Part X. Isolation of  Neutral Components from Glycosmis Arborea (Roxb.) DC.
author: S. C. Pakrashi
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description: A detailed isolation procedure of two isomeric triterpene alcohols of a new type, viz., (a) arborinol (= arborinol A), rn.p. 274°[α] D -+- 34.2 °  (CHCl 3 ) and (b)   iso-arborinol {=arborinol B), 298-99° [ \(\alpha\) ] D  +45° (CHCI 3 ), along with myricyl alcohol, m.p. 89.90°, stigmasterol and  \(\beta\) -sitosterol from the neutral frac­tion of the light petroleum extract of the leaves of Glycormis arborea (Roxb.) DC. has been described.
steps_raw: A detailed isolation procedure of two isomeric triterpene alcohols of a new type, viz., (a) arborinol (= arborinol A), rn.p. 274°[α] D -+- 34.2 °  (CHCl 3 ) and (b)   iso-arborinol {=arborinol B), 298-99° [ \(\alpha\) ] D  +45° (CHCI 3 ), along with myricyl alcohol, m.p. 89.90°, stigmasterol and  \(\beta\) -sitosterol from the neutral frac­tion of the light petroleum extract of the leaves of Glycormis arborea (Roxb.) DC. has been described.
